title: An Elegant Puzzle
subtitle: Systems of Engineering Management
author: Will Larson
themes: Systems thinking applied to engineering organizations
Managing technical debt and infrastructure investment
Hiring and onboarding as engineered systems
excerpt: A systems-oriented approach to engineering management — applying first principles, organizational design, and process thinking to the complex problems of leading engineering teams.
keyIdeas: Systems thinking applied to engineering organizations
Managing technical debt and infrastructure investment
keyTakeaways: Treat your organization as a system — design it deliberately rather than letting it evolve
Most management problems are systems problems, not people problems
Invest in infrastructure and tooling as a first-class concern
whoShouldRead: Anyone interested in the intersection of engineering and organizational design
whoShouldSkip: Junior engineers early in their career
Managers at very early-stage startups (pre-10 engineers)
